# Code App — Intelligent Work Layer Dashboard

## Overview

This is the **forward-architecture** React dashboard for the Intelligent Work Layer, built as a Power Platform Code App (Vite + React 18 + TypeScript). It replaces the PCF Virtual Control + Canvas App approach in `../src/AssistantDashboard/`.

See `../docs/code-app-migration.md` for the full migration rationale and status.

## Build & Dev

```bash
npm install
npm run dev          # Vite dev server on port 3000
npm run build        # tsc + vite build → dist/
npm run test         # vitest (199 tests, 18 files)
npm run test:watch   # vitest in watch mode
npm run test:coverage # vitest with 80% per-file threshold
npm run lint         # eslint src --ext .ts,.tsx

# Single test file:
npx vitest run src/components/__tests__/CardItem.test.tsx
```

## Deployment (requires Power Platform credentials)

```bash
npm run build
npx pac-sdk push     # or: pac code push
```

## Security and PII Hygiene

- Never commit real customer, partner, or tenant emails, domains, UPNs, org URLs, runtime config files, logs, dumps, or generated backup artifacts from local environments.
- Use `example.com` for all sample email addresses and domains in fixtures, mock data, prompts, docs, and test content.
- If a change touches any email-like value, run a PII scan before finalizing. The repo-level guardrail in `.github/workflows/prevent-pii-domains.yml` is the source of truth for the allowed tracked-file patterns.

## Key Architecture Differences from PCF

| Aspect | PCF (Legacy) | Code App (This) |
|--------|-------------|-----------------|
| Data source | PCF DataSet API (Canvas App passes records) | `CardDataService` interface → `useCards()` hook |
| React hosting | Platform-injected React 16 via `<platform-library>` | Bundled React 18 via Vite |
| Build tooling | `pcf-scripts build` (webpack) | `vite build` |
| Testing | Jest + jsdom | Vitest + jsdom |
| Output binding | PCF output properties → Canvas App OnChange | Direct service calls (mock or Dataverse) |

## Data Layer

- `src/services/CardDataService.ts` — Interface for card data providers
- `src/services/MockCardDataService.ts` — Mock implementation with fixture data
- `src/hooks/useCards.ts` — React hook wrapping the service

To connect to live Dataverse, implement `CardDataService` using the typed services generated by `pac-sdk add-data-source`.

## Adding a Data Source

```bash
pac code add-data-source -a <apiId> -c <connectionId>
# e.g.: pac code add-data-source -a "shared_commondataserviceforapps" -c "<your-connection-id>" -t "cr_assistantcards"
```

Generated models and services will appear in `src/Models` and `src/Services`.
